pi_items.source: Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) system major group 17-0000
Code analysis of setup_task.sh and bme280.c

privileged_info_summary: The task accurately reflects the role of a Computer Hardware Engineer (SOC 17-2061.00) working in the Architecture and Engineering major group. The synthetic scenario operates within an IoT/Smart Home manufacturing context. Code analysis confirms the presence of all claimed features: the task requires fixing embedded C drivers, hardware interfacing via an I2C mock, reading a provided datasheet, and resolving specific bugs involving bitwise operations (precedence), endianness (MSB/LSB swapped for humidity), and register maps (incorrect initialization sequence for control registers). Additionally, web searches verify that the BME280 device ID is truly 0x60, distinguishing it from the BMP280's 0x58.
